Attribute.name: wfActivityHelp
Attribute.AttrValue: This ADW step routes receipts to a Rule Group where rules can be set up to drive them to appropriate approvers based on amount and department, account code or other factors.

Attribute.name: wfActivitySupportNote
Attribute.AttrValue: If we have any background information for documentation purposes, we can put it within a SupportNote attribute like this. This is an ADW activity that checks a Rule Group to see whether a Receipt requires approval, and by whom. The client can create or manage rules and approvers for that Rule Group at any time, from the ADW tab.

Attribute.name: wfActivityHelp
Attribute.AttrValue: This activity checks a Rule Group to see if a receipt requires approval based on cost-related factors, and if so who should approve it.

Attribute.name: wfActivitySupportNote
Attribute.AttrValue: This static activity prompts an automated system action. The rule says to ALWAYS send receipts to the internal explicit folder called Create Inventory, which runs a robot called CreateInventoryRobot. (We named the folder, specified the robot and uploaded that with the accompanying Receipt WF Folders file.) This robot is built to create inventory receipts for any quantity receipt lines that have the Add to Inventory field set to true. If none are, nothing would happen but the step would still be marked complete. Note: This particular activity would ONLY be appropriate for clients who are using the Inventory Management module.

Attribute.name: wfActivityHelp
Attribute.AttrValue: This ADW activity prompts the system to create inventory items.
